changes: CRITICAL: EGA Al Taweelah smelter status upgraded to SHUTDOWN — power plant destroyed in Mar 29 strike; potlines solidified; zero output; WoodMac warns 3-3.5 Mtpa global loss (Bloomberg Apr 1). Added USAF F-15E Strike Eagle shot down over Iran Apr 3 (IRGC claimed F-35; debris shows F-15E from 494th FS). Added Zarif calls for Iran to 'declare victory and make deal' — first internal voice for settlement. Added Sheikh Khaled ADNOC resilience meeting (Apr 2). Updated Alba to ~30% capacity (Bloomberg). Added Kuwait Mar 30 power/desal plant attack (1 Indian worker killed). Added IDF killing of ballistic missile chief Atimi (Apr 3). Updated Brent to $99-$110 range. Updated Bahrain Sitra with 32 civilians injured (Rystad). Updated energy-news-data.json with Golden Pass first LNG, Russia gasoline ban, Aramco April cuts. No new FM declarations in last 48 hours.
newEvents: EGA Al Taweelah fully halted — power plant destroyed; potlines solidified; zero output; WoodMac warns 3-3.5 Mtpa global loss (Bloomberg Apr 1)
USAF F-15E Strike Eagle shot down over Iran Apr 3 — IRGC claimed F-35 but debris shows F-15E from 494th FS, RAF Lakenheath (The Aviationist)
Former FM Zarif calls for Iran to 'declare victory and make deal' — first internal voice for settlement (Al Jazeera Apr 3)
Sheikh Khaled chairs ADNOC executive resilience meeting Apr 2 — highest-level UAE leadership engagement (The National)
Alba smelter (Bahrain) confirmed at ~30% capacity after Mar 29 strike (Bloomberg Apr 1)
Kuwait power/desalination plant struck Mar 30 — 1 Indian worker killed (Al Jazeera, The National, Anadolu Agency)
IDF kills Makram Atimi, ballistic missile unit commander, in Kermanshah Apr 3 (JPost)
Bahrain Sitra Mar 9 attack: 32 civilians injured (Rystad Middle East Conflict dashboard)
Iran publishes bridge target lists for retaliation (Anadolu Agency Apr 3)
Philippines gets toll-free Hormuz passage — US ally cutting separate deal (Bloomberg Apr 2)
India waives customs duty on 40 petrochemical products until Jun 30 (OilPrice, BusinessToday Apr 2)
Italy calls for UN-led 'humanitarian corridor' for fertiliser — 30% of global fertilizer trade via Hormuz (CGTN Apr 2)
Golden Pass Train 1 (QatarEnergy/ExxonMobil JV) first LNG produced Mar 30 (OilPrice)
Russia bans gasoline exports from April 1 (Business Today)
Brent at $108.95 on Apr 3; intraday range $99.17-$109.72 (Trading Economics)
noNewFmReason: No new force majeure declarations found since Formosa Plastics Corp (fm-027, Apr 1). All 29 existing FM declarations remain active. Industry analysts warn April will see cascade of FM declarations as naphtha inventories deplete, particularly in Asia-Pacific petrochemical sector.
